scenario: You are designing a Power Apps canvas app for a healthcare organization. The organization has no professional developers. You create low code/no code components in the canvas app such as a welcome screen, a header, and a footer. You need to reuse those components in other canvas apps, enabling the apps to easily update the components in the other apps when they are updated.
why: Use a Component library. A component library is a reusable, no-code collection of canvas components (like the welcome screen, header and footer) that can be shared across many canvas apps; when you publish an update to the library, the apps that use it are prompted to pick up the updated version - meeting the 'easily update in other apps' requirement. A plain Component lives inside a single app (not reusable across apps that way), a PCF control requires pro-code (there are no developers), and an IFrame just embeds external web content.
